questions.question:
Match the LIST-I with LIST-II

LIST-I
A. Planck's constant
B. Stopping potential
C. Work function
D. Threshold frequency

LIST-II
I. ML²T²
II. T⁺¹
III. ML²T⁻¹
IV. ML²T⁻³A⁻¹

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
[h] = [E]/[ν] = ML²T⁻²/T⁻¹ = ML²T⁻¹
Stopping potential: E = q.V₀ => [V₀] = [E]/[q] = ML²T⁻²/AT = ML²T⁻³A⁻¹
Work function (φ) -> Energy form => [φ] = ML²T⁻²
Threshold freq. [ν₀] = T⁻¹

questions.question:
Two cars A and B are moving in the same
direction along a straight line with speeds 100 km/h and 80 km/h, respectively such that car A is moving ahead of car B. A person in car B throws a stone with a speed v so that it hits the car A with a speed of 5m/s.
The value of v is
questions.solution:
Vsa = 5 m/s = 18 km/hr
Vs - Va = Vsa
Vs = V + 80
V + 80 - 100 = 18 km/hr
V - 20 = 18
V = 38 km/hr

questions.question:
At t=0, a body of mass 100 g starts moving under the influence of a force (5î + 10ĵ)N.
After 2s its position is (2xî + 5yĵ)m. The ratio x : y is
questions.solution:
a = F/m = (5î + 10ĵ)/0.1 = (50î + 100ĵ)
s = ut + ½at²
s = 0 + ½(50î + 100ĵ)(2)²
s = 100î + 200ĵ = r = 2xî + 5yĵ
2x = 100 => x = 50
5y = 200 => y = 100 (from given solution text)
x/y = 5/4

questions.question:
If x and y coordinates of a projectile as a function of time (t) are given as 24t and 43.6t - 4.9t² respectively, then the angle (in degrees) made by the projectile
with horizontal when t=2 is
The height in terms of radius of the earth (R), at which the acceleration due to gravity becomes g/9 where g is acceleration due to gravity on earth's surface, is

A metal string A is suspended from a rigid support and its free end is attached to a block of mass M. Second block having mass 2M is suspended at the bottom of the first block using a string B. The area of cross sections of strings A and B are same.
The ratio of lengths of strings of A to B is 2 and the ratio of their Young's moduli (Ya/Yb) is 0.5.
The ratio of elongations in A to B is
questions.solution:
Δl = Tl/YA
Δl₁ = 3mgl/YA
Δl₂ = 2mg(l/2)/(2YA) = mgl/2YA
Δl₁/Δl₂ = 6

questions.question:
A water spray gun is attached to a hose of cross sectional area 30 cm².
The gun comprises of 10 perforations each of cross sectional area of 15 mm².
If the water flows in the hose with the speed of 50 cm/s, calculate the speed at which the water flows out from each perforation.
(Neglect any edge effects)
questions.solution:
Using equation of continuity
AV = 10av
v = AV/10a = (30 × 50) / (10 × 0.15) = 1000 cm/s = 10 m/s

questions.question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R
Assertion A: If the average kinetic energy of H₂ and O₂ molecules, kept in two different sized containers are same, then their temperatures will be same.
Reason R: The r.m.s.
speed of H₂ and O₂ molecules are same at same temperature.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.solution:
Avg.
kinetic energy of molecule will be (f/2)KT. But vrms = √(3RT/M).
Molar mass M is different so vrms is different.

questions.question:
The temperature of a metal strip having coefficient of linear expansion α is increased from T₁ to T₂ resulting in increase of its length by ΔL₁.
The temperature is further increased from T₂ to T₃ such that the increase in its length is ΔL₂.
Given T₃ + T₁ = 2T₂ and T₂ - T₁ = ΔT, the value of ΔL₂ is
questions.solution:
ΔL₁ = L₀α(T₂ - T₁)
So final length at T₂ = L₀ + ΔL₁
ΔL₂ = (L₀ + ΔL₁)α(T₃ - T₂) and T₃ - T₂ = T₂ - T₁ = ΔT
ΔL₂ = L₀αΔT + ΔL₁αΔT
= ΔL₁ + ΔL₁αΔT = ΔL₁(1 + αΔT)

questions.question:
A uniform disc of radius R and
mass M is free to oscillate about the axis A as shown in the figure.
For small oscillations the time period is (g is acceleration due to gravity)
questions.solution:
Idisc = mR²/4 + mR² = 5mR²/4
For small displacement τ = -mgRθ
α = -(mgR / (5mR²/4))θ = -(4g/5R)θ
ω = √(4g/5R)
T = 2π√(5R/4g)

questions.question:
A rigid dipole undergoes a simple harmonic motion about its centre in the presence of an electric field E₁ = E₀x̂.
If another electric field E₂ = 2E₀(ŷ + ẑ) is introduced to the system, what will be the percentage change in the frequency of the oscillation (approximate)?
questions.solution:
Initial freq.
fi = (1/2π)√(P(E₀)/I)
Final EF = E₀î + 2E₀(ĵ + k̂)
Magnitude = 3E₀
Final Freq.
ff = (1/2π)√(P(3E₀)/I)
%change = Δf/fi = (√3 - 1) × 100 ≈ 73%

questions.question:
A solenoid has a core made of material with relative permeability 400. The magnetic field produced in the interior of solenoid is 1.0T.
The magnetic intensity is SI units is α × 10⁵.
The value of α is (Free space permeability μ₀ = 4π × 10⁻⁷ SI units).

A magnetic field vector in an electromagnetic wave is represented by, B = B₀sin(2πνt - 2πx/λ)ĵ.
Its associated electric field vector is
questions.solution:
Ê × B̂ = Ĉ
B̂ = ĵ, Ĉ = î
Ê = -k̂
E₀/B₀ = c = νλ => E₀ = B₀νλ
E = -νλB₀sin(2πνt - 2πx/λ)k̂

questions.question:
A convex lens is made from glass material having refractive index of 1.4 with same radius of curvature on both sides.
The ratio of its focal length and radius of curvature is

An unpolarized light of certain intensity passes through a combination of two polarizers whose transmission axes are at 30° and 90° respectively, with respect to the horizontal axis.
A third polarizer with its transmission axis at 60° with the horizontal axis is placed between the two existing polarizers.
The ratio of the output intensities with and without the third polarizer is
questions.solution:
I₀ -> I₀/2
Without 3rd: (I₀/2)cos²(60°) = I₀/8
With 3rd: (I₀/2)cos²(30°)cos²(30°) = (I₀/2) × (3/4) × (3/4) = 9I₀/32
Ratio = (9I₀/32) / (I₀/8) = 9/4

(B) Alpha particle and gold nucleus have equal charge.
(C) The impact parameter is minimum for a few alpha particles.
(D) A few alpha particles have very high kinetic energy.
(E) Only a few
alpha particles undergo head-on collision with the nuclei.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:

Assertion A: A diode under reverse- biased condition provides very small current which is nearly independent of voltage until a critical limit at which the current increases drastically.
Reason R: Below the critical voltage limit, only majority charge carriers flow which increases drastically above critical voltage.

questions.question: A diode has Zener voltage of 10V and maximum power dissipation of 0.5W, then the minimum resistance to be used in series with this diode for safety when it is connected to a 25V power supply is ____ Ω.
questions.solution:
Maximum current from diode
i = P/V = 0.5/10 = 0.05A
Now let
resistance is R
25 = 0.05 × R + 10
R = 15/0.05 = 300 Ω

The farthest distance the bullets could reach is 6.4 m.
The speed of the bullets from the gun is ____ m/s.
